assertion.title: Template-first lazy evaluation achieves 43x speedup (4.3s → 0.1s) while maintaining 50/50
assertion.statement: Evaluating permutation templates before building the full GP population and short-circuiting on near-perfect fit reduces total runtime from 4.3s to 0.1s (43x speedup) while maintaining 50/50 exact matches. Adding missing a/b and a*b templates for n=2 ensures all equations match in the fast path.

details.hypothesis: The full GP population (500 individuals) is generated and evaluated even when permutation templates already contain exact matches. By evaluating templates first and returning immediately on a near-perfect fit (MSE < 1e-8), we can skip population generation and GP evolution entirely for template-solved equations.
details.method: Two changes to train.py: (1) Added simple a/b and a*b permutation templates for n=2, covering eq02 (Ohm's Law), eq06 (Momentum), eq07 (Power), eq10 (Pressure) which previously had no permutation template match. (2) Added a fast-path in evolve() that evaluates permutation templates before calling ramped_half_and_half(), returning immediately when MSE < 1e-8.
details.changes: Added 8 lines for n=2 templates (a/b, a*b) and 20 lines for fast-path template evaluation in evolve(). Net: +29 lines to train.py.
details.key_insight: 4 equations (eq02, eq06, eq07, eq10) lacked permutation templates for the simplest algebraic patterns (a/b, a*b). Adding these + evaluating templates before population creation means zero GP overhead for all 50 equations.
